findings.evidence: `omit [IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A] in` at line 52 explicitly omits both section variables for the companion theorem, confirming they are unused by the definition and proof.
findings.fix: Move `IsUnipotent` outside `section Unipotent` (keeping it at the outer `variable (k A) [Field k] [CommRing A] [HopfAlgebra k A]` level) and drop the `omit` annotation, which becomes unnecessary.
findings.issue: `IsUnipotent` is in `section Unipotent` which adds `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]`, but neither appears in the definition body. The `omit [IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A] in` on line 52 is direct evidence both are spurious. The natural level is the outer `variable`'s `[CommRing A] [HopfAlgebra k A]`.

findings.evidence: Body is `h.unipotent_radical_trivial` where `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` (line 65); `True` is provable without `h`.
findings.fix: Remove this theorem entirely (it is subsumed by `trivial`) until `unipotent_radical_trivial` is replaced with actual mathematical content; do not export a theorem with an unused typeclass assumption.
findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` has hypothesis `[h : ReductiveGroup k A]` but its conclusion is `True`, making the hypothesis unused — `trivial` proves `True` with no assumptions at all.

findings.evidence: Body is `SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`, the exact projection Lean generates from the `extends` clause.
findings.fix: Delete the explicit instance; the auto-generated `SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup` is already an instance.
findings.issue: `SemisimpleGroup.instReductiveGroup` re-provides the instance `SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup` that Lean 4 auto-generates from `class SemisimpleGroup extends ReductiveGroup k A` at line 68.

summary: Three generality issues: `IsUnipotent` is scoped under unused `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]` section variables; `unipotentRadical_trivial'` carries an unused typeclass hypothesis; and `instReductiveGroup` redeclares an auto-derived `extends` instance.
